Current state: 12 tests passing
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_22-55-29_claim-office-lite-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-portkey-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Green after completion.
You MUST output the full Step 7 block verbatim (both Compilation
Prediction and Runtime Prediction lines, each with Correct or
Incorrect). Do not abbreviate. Do not collapse the two prediction
lines into one.

Note: Need to construct a scenario where a claim payout is 350.5 G before rounding. Half-payment rule (ench>=8) on dragon material: payout = damage*0.5 - 100 deductible, must = 350.5. So damage*0.5 = 450.5 → damage = 901. Make a quote with a dragon-material sword ench 8, then a claim with damage 901. Expected payout 350.

Construct scenario:
{
  customer: { yearsWithMHPCO: 0 },
  steps: [
    { op: "quote", items: [{ type: "sword", material: "dragon", enchantment: 8 }] },
    { op: "claim", policy: 0, incident: { cause: "fire", damages: [{ itemType: "sword", amount: 901 }] } }
  ]
}
Expected results[1] = { payout: 350 }.

(High-ench applies since ench >= 8, gives 50% reimbursement = 450.5; minus 100 deductible = 350.5; rounded down to 350.)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the test file and implementation to understand the current state.

Now I'll activate the test. I'll convert the `it.todo` to an executable test with the scenario described.

### Step 1: Activate the Test

input.old_string: it.todo("payout calculation yielding 350.5 G rounds DOWN to 350 G (MHPCO favor)");

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_22-55-29_claim-office-lite-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-7-portkey-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.spec.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
### Step 2: Make Prediction - Compilation Error

Looking at the current `Scenario` type, it requires `items: Item[]` on every step. A "claim" step doesn't have `items`, it has `policy` and `incident`. However, since `runScenario` accepts `unknown` and casts it via `as Scenario`, the actual call site won't have a TS compile error — the test passes its argument as `unknown` to `runScenario`. Also `Item` type doesn't have `material`, but inline object literals against `Item[]` typed parameter may flag excess properties... but since the whole thing is passed to `runScenario(scenario: unknown)`, TypeScript will not check the object literal against `Item[]`.

So actually, there will be NO compilation error. The test will run and produce a runtime error directly. Let me re-examine.

The argument is passed to `runScenario(scenario: unknown)`. Object literals against `unknown` parameter are allowed freely. So no compile error.

The runtime behavior: `s.steps.map` will try to compute `computeQuotePremium` on the claim step. The claim step has no `items` field. So `step.items` will be `undefined`, then `countItemsByType(undefined)` will fail with "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'Symbol(Symbol.iterator)')" or similar in the `for...of` loop.

🔴 Red Phase - Compilation Error Prediction:
- Test: "payout calculation yielding 350.5 G rounds DOWN to 350 G (MHPCO favor)"
- Expected: NO compilation error (runScenario accepts `unknown`)
- Reason: The scenario argument is typed `unknown`, so object literal shape isn't checked
- Error: None expected at compile time

### Step 3: Run Test - Verify Compilation

content.text:
Confirmed: No compilation error. Direct runtime error: "items is not iterable" because `runScenario` treats claim steps as quote steps. This is a "wrong reason" failure — the function `claim` handling doesn't exist yet, which is the equivalent of the compilation stage.

### Step 4: Create Empty Function (Stub for claim handling)

I need to add minimal handling so claim steps don't crash, returning an empty payout (e.g., `undefined`). This is the analog of stub creation.
